data.title: Brazil's Lula says he and Trump have a lot to talk about
data.description:
Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva told reporters on Wednesday that he hoped he and U.S. President Donald Trump would mend the relationship between the two countries if they meet in a few days, as they agreed to on Tuesday, during a conversation at the United Nations General Assembly in New York.

"I made a point of telling President Trump that we have a lot to talk about," Lula said, though he declined to share more details about what would be offered in negotiations.

Trump imposed 50% tariffs on most Brazilian goods in July, sanctioned a Supreme Court judge, and took away the visas of several government officials in retaliation for what he called a "witch hunt" against former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, who was sentenced to 27 years in prison for plotting a coup to remain in power after he lost the 2022 elections.

But, after their seconds-long surprise meeting in New York on Tuesday, Trump invited Lula to a conversation the following week, arguing the two of them had "excellent chemistry," which was "a good sign."

Lula said he believed Trump's decision to retaliate against Brazil stemmed from the "quality of information" he had about the country.

"I believe he is ill-informed about Brazil," Lula said about Trump. "Possibly that's what led him to make some unacceptable decisions," Lula said.

data.title: First Bonus Prize in the Great Canadian Treasure Hunt Discovered in Dawson City
data.description:
Over 61,000 Canadians join the hunt for gold as first $30K bonus prize is found in Dawson City, Yukon

Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - September 24, 2025) - The Great Canadian Treasure Hunt has its first winner. The inaugural Bonus Prize, six one-ounce gold coins valued at over $30,000 was found this week in Dawson City, Yukon.

The discovery marks a thrilling milestone in the national treasure hunt, which offers Canadians the chance to uncover more than $1.3 million in gold hidden across the country.

The winning treasure hunter, Gina Anderson, said the find capped off days of research, puzzle-solving, and a journey that ultimately led to Dawson City's Bonanza Creek discovery claim site.

"I was born and raised in the Yukon, so Dawson City already feels like home," said Anderson. "With a bit of local knowledge and a clue, I had that aha moment when everything came together. Finding real gold felt almost unreal at first, but the hunt gave me a deeper appreciation for Canada's mining history and the thrill of discovery."

A full video interview with Gina is now available on The Northern Miner's official YouTube channel and embedded below.

Cannot view this video? Visit:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=leY5-Cc_8TI

The Bonus Prize is the first of twelve monthly Bonus Prizes in addition to the Grand Prize: 217 one-ounce gold coins minted from Agnico Eagle's Detour Lake Mine and valued today at over $1.1 million.

"The Dawson City discovery is just the beginning! The Grand Prize and monthly Bonus Prizes are still out there," said Anthony Vaccaro, President of The Northern Miner Group. "We're thrilled to see Canadians across the country engaging with Canada's mining heritage in such an adventurous way."

When first announced, each Bonus Prize was valued at $27,729. Today, that same prize is worth more than $30,000, reflecting gold's steady climb. The Grand Prize has also risen in value, now worth over $1.1 million. These increases underscore gold's enduring role as a hedge against currency debasement and a reminder of why precious metals remain essential in uncertain times.

Details for the next Bonus Prize location will be announced shortly, keeping the momentum of the hunt alive. Treasure seekers are encouraged to subscribe to The Northern Miner and follow updates on CEO.CA, MINING.COM, and official social media channels for upcoming clues.

Monthly Prizes Connect to the $1M+ Gold Hunt

Eleven more bonus prizes (each representing six one-ounce gold coins) will roll out over the next year, revealed through new monthly stories and videos. Every release carries two layers of mystery: clues pointing to the regional bonus prize and the $1+ million grand treasure. Together, these releases will ignite fresh debates and theories while inspiring people to reconnect with the country's history of adventure and discovery.

Join the hunt at:

treasure.northernminer.com

This campaign is proudly presented with the support of industry sponsors including Agnico Eagle Mines Limited, Sprott Money, EarthLabs Inc., IAMGOLD Corporation, Kinross Gold Corporation, The World Gold Council, McEwen Inc., Alamos Gold Inc., Ernst & Young LLP, Mining Matters, MINING.COM, CEO.CA and The Canadian Mining Journal.

For more information, including full contest rules, FAQs and updates, visit treasure.northernminer.com.

Follow @northernminer (X/FB/YouTube) | @thenorthernminer (IG) | @mining (X) | @miningdotcom (IG/FB/YouTube); @ceodotca (X/IG/FB/TikTok) | @ceocafilm (YouTube) for ongoing clues and community updates.

-30-

data.title: Review & Preview: The September Rally Hits a Snag — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Connor Smith

Slipping. The stock market's September to remember has hit its first real snag.

The S&P 500 dropped 0.3%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 172 points, or 0.4%. The Nasdaq Composite fell 0.3%.

It marked the first time all three indexes fell together for two days in a row since Sept. 2. The fact that it's been so long since a broad market slide defies historical trends for September.

The S&P 500 has averaged a September decline of 1.1% going back to 1928, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The Nasdaq has averaged a decline of 0.9% during Septembers going back to 1971. The Dow has fallen 1.1% during the month going back to 1897. It's the worst calendar month for all three historically.

So far this September, the S&P is up 2.8%, while the Nasdaq is up 4.9%. The Dow is up 1.3%. The S&P and Nasdaq are having their best Septembers since 2013.

"The stock market's sustained push during the traditionally weak seasonal period appears to be fueling 'bubble' talk, especially in regard to tech," writes Daniel Skelly, head of Morgan Stanley's Wealth Management Market Research & Strategy Team. "While even the strongest rallies inevitably experience retracements, and the market certainly faces ongoing policy and economic uncertainties, there are good reasons to believe this talk is misplaced."

Skelly writes that there have been five bull markets that lasted more than two years over the past 50 years, adding that the average length has been eight years. Compared with that, we're only just nearing year three of this bull market.

Frank Cappelleri, founder of technical analysis firm CappThesis also made the case that this latest skid is a "normal pullback" after the market appeared to be short-term extended.

Looking at the S&P 500's relative strength index moved into overbought territory for the fourth time since the S&P's April lows, but Cappelleri notes the prior three times the market's drawdowns were "minimal at best."

Whether a bigger decline is coming depends on if the next dip is bought, and, if so, how the market bounces, he argues.

"Buyers have been repeatedly and instantly rewarded with new highs soon after buying prior dips," Cappelleri says. "If and when that does not happen, and instead a lower high results, that could mark a shift in momentum from the decidedly positive trend of the past five months to something more negative. Those are all a few steps ahead, but it's a scenario worth watching as the market continues higher."

The Hot Stock: Xcel Energy +6.7% The Biggest Loser: Freeport-McMoRan -17.0%

Best Sector: Energy +1.3% Worst Sector: Materials -1.2%

1-800-GAMBLER Has a Problem

Gambling problem? You've probably heard of the helpline to call.

But 1-800-GAMBLER is in turmoil after a New Jersey court ruled the national group fighting gambling addiction must give the helpline back to a locally run nonprofit that launched the number in 1983, my Barron's colleague Nick Devor reports. Nick writes:

The number has become ubiquitous across U.S. advertising for casinos and sportsbooks, which urge responsible gambling alongside their advertising. Calls to the helpline have soared since the National Council on Problem Gambling licensed control of the number from its New Jersey affiliate, the Council on Compulsive Gambling of New Jersey, in June 2022.

But local problem gambling groups have been increasingly at odds with the well-funded National Council, which calls itself "gambling neutral" and partners with casinos and sportsbooks like DraftKings and FanDuel.

Nick reports that the National Council has invested heavily into operating the helpline over the last three years, which included bringing online nationwide text and chat functions. They've also developed new standards and data collection efforts for the helpline and advertised the number.

On the flip side, problem-gambling groups in Florida and New Mexico that Nick reports have pushed back against some universal standards set by the National Council have seen calls rerouted away from local helplines in those states to a call center in Louisiana. The ruling will give routing control back to the New Jersey council, Nick reports.

Nick writes:

During the hearing, James Tarnofsky, a lawyer for the National Council on Problem Gambling, said that returning control of the helpline number to the New Jersey group would be complex.

"The entire network is under the domain of National — the texting, the chatting, in 49 states and territories," he said at Monday's hearing. "It's not really 'Just flip a switch and it goes to CCGNJ.' The network will fall apart for a period of time. People will be without the services."

"Certain functions based on proprietary systems will cease to work right away," Tarnofsky later told Barron's.

You can read the full story here.

The Calendar

Accenture, CarMax, Costco Wholesale, and Jabil report quarterly results tomorrow.

The Bureau of Economic Analysis will release its third and final estimate of second-quarter gross-domestic-product growth. GDP is expected to have grown at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 3.3%, unchanged from the BEA's second estimate.

The Census Bureau will release durable goods report for August, and the National Association of Realtors reports existing-home sales for August.

What We're Reading Today

  • Marvell Stock Has Been Hot. Now the AI Chip Maker Is Buying Back Shares.
  • Buy This Stock on the Dip. Shares Can Rise Nearly 40%.
  • Alibaba Soars on AI Spending Hike. Why the Stock Can Climb Even Higher.
  • Freeport-McMoRan Says Its Gold and Copper Sales Will Drop. The Stock Is Falling.
  • Barron's Exclusive: FIS Acquires Goldman Sachs-Backed Start-Up Amount

Barron's Live returns on Monday. Barron's Live features timely and actionable insights for investors. We give you behind-the-scenes conversations with the newsroom, connecting you with our editors and reporters covering the markets, the economy, and more.

Sign up here.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Energy Climbs With Oil Futures - Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies rose alongside oil futures as Ukrainian attacks on Russian energy facilities stoked supply concerns.

Russia may slow diesel exports after attacks on refineries, analysts said.

French Energy major TotalEnergies said it won a $5.3 billion tender from the national government as part of a consortium to build and operate France's largest offshore wind farm, even as Germany's RWE said it intended to exit the partnership.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Materials Decline as Gold Rally Stalls - Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell as a record-breaking run for gold stalled.

Gold futures fell 1.3%, their largest retreat in more than a month, but remained close to all-time highs, above $3700 an ounce.

A super typhoon in Hong Kong delayed a blockbuster initial public offering by the gold unit of one of China's biggest miners, Zijin Mining.

Canadian miner Lithium Americas is in negotiations with the U.S. Energy Department and automaker General Motors over the terms of its $2.26 billion government loan for a large lithium mining project in Nevada. The U.S. earlier this year bought a stake in the producer of another critical mineral for technology, electric-car manufacturing and other applications — rare-earth producer MP Materials.

Two workers were found dead last weekend and five remain missing after a mud slide earlier this month at one of Freeport-McMoRan's copper and gold mines in Indonesia, the American miner warned. The massive mud slide will halt production at the Grasberg mining facility likely weighing on production and sales this year, Freeport warned. Shares of the miner incurred one of their biggest selloffs since the 2020 pandemic.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Industrials Decline on Growth Doubts - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies fell amid concerns about the economic growth outlook.

China said it would no longer seek new special treatment for developing countries in current and future World Trade Organization negotiations, signaling a possible effort to ease a longstanding friction point with the U.S. ahead of a planned summit.

Japanese auto giant Honda Motor ended production of its Acura ZDX electric SUV.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Health Care Slips as Regulatory Fears Linger - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked down as regulatory fears lingered.

Shares of Tylenol maker Kenvue continued their retreat amid fears that pregnant women would heed recent warnings from President Trump and the Department of Health and Human Services about possible links between the painkiller and autism risks in children.

Shares of pharmaceutical equipment manufacturer Gerresheimer lost more than one-third of their value after Germany's financial watchdog said the company had violated accounting regulations.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: First United Hikes Dividend 18%
data.description:
By Terell Wright

First United boosted its dividend by 18% to 26 cents per share from 22 cents.

The new payout, equal to $1.04 per year, represents an annual yield of about 2.8%, based on Wednesday's closing price of $37.57.

The commercial banking company on Wednesday said the new dividend will be paid on Nov. 3 to common stockholders as of the close of business on Oct. 17.

Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com

data.title: Consumer Cos Climb After New-Home Sales Data - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rose after surprisingly strong new-home sales data.

The sales rate for new single-family homes rose by more than 20% to 800,000 in August, the Commerce Department, as builder promotions lured home buyers.

In a bad sign for discretionary spending, recreational-vehicle maker Thor Industries warned a challenging macroeconomic environment could affect fiscal 2026. RVs are often viewed as a leading indicator of discretionary spending because they are often the first things that disappear from consumers' budgets when money gets tight.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Financials Losses Limited Amid Deal-Making, Strong Home Sales - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions fell, but not by as much as the broad market, as elevated deal and new-home buying activity boded well for the sector.

The sales rate for new single-family homes in August was 800,000, the Commerce Department reported.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. is in talks to provide Argentina with a $20 billion swap line to stave off a currency crisis and support President Javier Milei's free-market overhaul.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Chile to curb budget growth as Codelco ramps up recovery, minister says
data.description:
Key points:
  • 2026 budget growth limited compared to previous years, Grau states
  • Grau confident in Codelco's strategy despite past production challenges
  • Unemployment rate remains a priority for Chile's finance minister

By Fabian Cambero

Chile will have a "responsible" 2026 budget with a limited increase while state-run Codelco, the world's largest copper miner, seeks to recover from recent production problems, the country's new finance minister told Reuters on Wednesday.

In an interview at his office in Santiago, Nicolas Grau, appointed in August by his close ally President Gabriel Boric, said the 2026 budget due in Congress would have a more "limited" spending increase than in recent years.

The specific amount will be announced by Boric, but the budget increase has been on a downward trend since 2023 when it grew 4.2%. Grau said that with this budget, the average annual increase would be about 2%.

"What we're going to do is define a responsible budget with Congress that addresses the country's needs, and provides sufficient flexibility to the next administration," he said. Chile is facing a presidential election in November and Boric, who will serve until March, is not allowed to run for consecutive re-election.

CODELCO'S COPPER RECOVERY

Chile produces about a third of the world's copper, its main source of income. State-owned Codelco has been struggling to boost output for years. In August, it cut its annual production target due to a fatal accident at its flagship El Teniente mine.

Grau emphasized his confidence in Codelco's strategy to close production gaps in previous years, and highlighted initiatives like its partnership with Anglo American to exploit two neighboring deposits.

"(Codelco) is creating these public-private partnerships that allow it to maintain its leadership in the copper sector," Grau said.

SPEEDING UP PERMITTING TO BOOST INVESTMENT

Grau said he was confident the economy would grow 2.5% this year, boosted by an investment increase of more than 5% for carbon-neutral projects like clean energy and digital infrastructure.

Rising unemployment has also been one of Grau's priorities for the final months of the administration. The country's former labor minister, Jeannette Jara, is the leftist government's coalition candidate in the upcoming election.

"Although the unemployment rate has dropped somewhat in the latest data, 8.7% is a challenging unemployment rate. We believe that rate must be reduced," Grau said.

He added that policies like pension reform and improved permitting - a key demand from large miners like BHP and renewable energy companies like TotalEnergies - have allowed for greater economic growth.

Still, he acknowledged that less progress has been made in reforming environmental permitting, a sector that takes the longest compared to other categories.

He argued that the executive branch had made "significant efforts" to move the reform forward but could not predict whether it would be approved during this administration.

data.title: Slovak lawmakers approve measures in 2.7 bln euro deficit-reduction package
data.description:
Slovakia's parliament on Wednesday evening approved measures in the government's 2.7 billion euro package of tax hikes and savings aimed at reducing one of the European Union's widest fiscal gaps.

Finance Minister Ladislav Kamenicky announced the deficit-reduction package earlier in September as the government seeks to cut the state budget gap to at least 4.3% of gross domestic product in 2026, down from 4.7% targeted this year.

The package is the third under Prime Minister Robert Fico's leftist-nationalist government, which wants to keep up social spending such as on caps on energy prices and an extra pension payment for retirees.

The government has blamed higher deficits and debt increases on previous administrations in Slovakia, which entered the EU's excessive-deficit procedure last year.

The measures approved on Wednesday include reducing some public holidays, a higher tax on foods with high sugar or salt levels, an increase in workers' compulsory health insurance payments, and tax bracket changes raising more revenue.

In total, changes should bring 1.44 billion euros to the budget next year, the ministry said in material submitted to parliament.

The government is also seeking 1.3 billion euros in savings.

The government last year had also approved a package of taxes and savings which was worth 2.7 billion euros.

In April, the finance ministry said trade risks and geopolitical factors will cause a one-year delay, to 2028, in Slovakia's aim of getting its deficit below the EU-mandated 3% of GDP limit.

data.title: Live cattle drop as beef prices, packer margins erode
data.description:
By Karl Plume

Chicago Mercantile Exchange cattle futures fell on Wednesday on long liquidation by funds and as sinking beef prices and eroding packer margins weighed on the market.

Feeder cattle futures also retreated in a technical correction after four consecutive sessions of gains that were fueled by concerns over tightening U.S. supplies.

"We are seeing more pushback from consumers on high retail beef prices and packers are scaling back their coverage as a result. This led to a weak boxed beef market today, pressuring futures," said Karl Setzer, partner at Consus Ag Consulting.

CME December live cattle (LCZ25) ended 1.925 cents lower at 236.825 cents per pound. October feeder cattle (FCV25) ended at 359.625 cents per pound, down 2.500 cents after hitting a three-week high in the prior session on worries about tight supplies following news that Mexico had confirmed a case of New World screwworm, a damaging livestock pest, less than 70 miles from the U.S. border.

Beef prices have been falling from recent peaks as high prices dent demand and as cooler weather cuts consumer purchases for cookouts.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture said the choice boxed beef cutout (BEEF-US-CH) fell by $3.41 to $377.39 per hundredweight, while the select cutout (BEEF-US-SE) was down $3.48 at $356.42 per cwt.

Meanwhile, elevated cash cattle prices and sinking beef values further eroded processing margins as beef packers were estimated to be losing $132.70 per head on Wednesday, compared to losses of $52.25 per head a week ago and profits of $46.00 per head two weeks ago, according to livestock marketing advisory service HedgersEdge.

In the pork market, lean hogs posted losses in a fund-selling retreat from contract highs notched in the prior session, with December futures (LHV25) down 2.600 cents at 88.125 cents per pound.

Analysts expect Thursday's quarterly USDA hog herd report to show September 1 U.S. supplies about steady from a year earlier, according to a Reuters poll.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: U.S. Stocks End Lower as Tech Continues to Recede | New-home ...
data.description:

data.title: ORBIT GARANT GENERATES HIGHEST NET EARNINGS IN MORE THAN TEN YEARS IN FISCAL 2025
data.description:
(21:07 GMT) *Orbit Garant Drilling 4Q EPS C$0.06 >OGD.T
(21:07 GMT) *Orbit Garant Drilling 4Q Net C$2.2M >OGD.T
(21:07 GMT) *Orbit Garant Drilling 4Q Rev C$47.2M >OGD.T
(21:07 GMT) Press Release: ORBIT GARANT GENERATES HIGHEST NET EARNINGS IN MORE THAN TEN YEARS IN FISCAL 2025

VAL-D'OR, QC, Sept. 24, 2025 /CNW/ - Orbit Garant Drilling Inc. ("Orbit Garant" or the "Company") today announced its financial results for the three-month period ("Q4 2025") and fiscal year ended June 30, 2025. All dollar amounts are in Canadian dollars unless otherwise stated.

Financial Highlights

 
($ amounts in   Three months    Three months    Year endedJune  Year endedJune

Gross Profit               7.6             7.5            28.3            21.2

(2) As adjusted. See note 2 to the audited consolidated 
 financial statements

"Staying the course with our strategic plan, we generated strong year-over-year growth in earnings for the year, driven by the improved profitability of our operations in both Canada and South America," said Daniel Maheu, President and CEO of Orbit Garant. "Our positive results are attributable to our focus on senior and well-financed intermediate customers in Canada and South America, our disciplined approach in our business strategy, and continued execution of our operational improvement program supported by strong customer demand. Our strong growth in cash flow from operations provided us with the flexibility to both significantly reduce our Credit Facility debt during the year and buy back shares through our normal course issuer bid. These capital allocation activities will help drive further value creation for shareholders."

"The price of gold is currently at historically high levels, and the price of copper also remains strong, which provides a strong incentive for our customers to continue to invest in mine exploration and development activities. Looking ahead, we intend on staying disciplined in our business strategy, keep delivering on our operational improvement program, continuing to pay down debt to strengthen our balance sheet and remain focused on our core regions, Canada and South America. We still have significant operational capacity to service new drilling contracts in Canada and a potential to expand our regional presence with minimal mobilization costs."

Fourth Quarter Results

Revenue for Q4 2025 totalled $47.2 million, an increase of 3.9% compared to $45.3 million for the three-month period ended June 30, 2024 ("Q4 2024"). Canada revenue totalled $33.8 million in Q4 2025, an increase of 2.7% compared to $32.8 million in Q4 2024, reflecting higher revenue per metre drilled. International revenue totalled $13.4 million in Q4 2025, an increase of 7.0% compared to $12.5 million in Q4 2024, reflecting increased drilling activity in South America.

Gross profit for Q4 2025 was $7.6 million, or 16.0% of revenue, compared to $7.5 million, or 16.6% of revenue, in Q4 2024. Adjusted gross margin(1), excluding depreciation expenses and a gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment, was 20.2% in Q4 2025, compared to 17.8% in Q4 2024. The increases in gross profit and adjusted gross margin(1) were primarily attributable to higher revenue per metre drilled in Canada, increased drilling activity in South America and the cessation of drilling activities in West Africa during Q2 2024, which were unprofitable.

General and Administrative expenses were $4.2 million, or 9.0% of revenue, in Q4 2025, compared to $4.3 million, or 9.4% of revenue, in Q4 2024.

Adjusted EBITDA(1) totalled $5.5 million in Q4 2025 compared to $6.7 million in Q4 2024. The decrease was primarily attributable to an unfavourable foreign exchange variance and start-up costs for a new project in South America, partially offset by increased operating earnings in Canada.

Net earnings for Q4 2025 were $2.2 million, or $0.06 per share (diluted), compared to a net loss of $2.3 million, or $0.06 per share (diluted), in Q4 2024. The Company's net earnings in Q4 2025 were primarily attributable to the effect of the substantial modification of a receivable and expected credit loss and the foreign currency translation loss on disposal of subsidiaries in Q4 2024 related to the Company's exit from West Africa, partially offset by a slight reduction in consolidated operating earnings, a reduced income tax recovery and an unfavourable foreign exchange movement in Q4 2025 compared to Q4 2024.

Fiscal 2025 Results

Revenue in Fiscal 2025 totalled $189.1 million, an increase of 4.3% compared to $181.2 million for the year ended June 30, 2024 ("Fiscal 2024"). Canada revenue totalled $136.1 million for Fiscal 2025, an increase of 2.6% from $132.6 million in Fiscal 2024, reflecting higher revenue per metre drilled. International revenue for Fiscal 2025 increased by 9.0% to $53.0 million compared to $48.6 million in Fiscal 2024, reflecting increased drilling activity in South America.

Gross profit for Fiscal 2025 was $28.3 million, or 15.0% of revenue, compared to $21.2 million, or 11.7% of revenue, in Fiscal 2024. Adjusted gross margin(1), excluding depreciation expenses and a gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment, was 19.5% in Fiscal 2025, compared to adjusted gross margin(1), excluding depreciation expenses and a gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment, of 15.9% in Fiscal 2024. The increases in gross profit and adjusted gross margin(1) were primarily attributable to higher revenue per metre drilled in Canada, increased drilling activity in South America and the cessation of drilling activities in West Africa during Q2 2024, which were unprofitable.

General and Administrative expenses were $16.7 million, or 8.8% of revenue, in Fiscal 2025, compared to $16.4 million, or 9.1% of revenue, in Fiscal 2024. The increase in Fiscal 2025 reflects annual inflationary wage adjustments and increased marketing and promotional activities.

Adjusted EBITDA(1) totalled $21.7 million in Fiscal 2025 compared to $14.7 million in Fiscal 2024. The increase reflects higher operating earnings in both Canada and South America, and a favourable foreign exchange gain.

Net earnings for Fiscal 2025 were $7.5 million, or $0.20 per share (diluted), compared to a net loss of $2.4 million, or $0.06 per share (diluted), in Fiscal 2024. The Company's net earnings in Fiscal 2025 were primarily attributable to increased operating earnings in both Canada and South America, the effect of a substantial modification of a receivable and expected credit loss and the foreign currency translation loss on disposal of subsidiaries in Q4 2024, interest revenue, and a favourable foreign exchange gain, partially offset by increased income tax expense.

Liquidity and Capital Resources

The Company repaid a net amount of $7.5 million on its Credit Facility in Fiscal 2025, compared to a repayment of $0.7 million in Fiscal 2024. The Company's long-term debt under the Credit Facility, including an undrawn US$5.0 million revolving credit facility and the current portion, was $14.0 million as at June 30, 2025, compared to $21.5 million as at June 30, 2024.

On October 28, 2024, the Company announced that the Toronto Stock Exchange ("TSX") accepted its notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid (the "NCIB Program") to purchase outstanding common shares of Orbit Garant on the open market in accordance with the rules of the TSX. Pursuant to the NCIB Program, Orbit Garant may purchase, from time to time, in aggregate up to 1,868,637 common shares over a 12-month period commencing on October 31, 2024, and terminating on October 30, 2025. During Fiscal 2025, the Company repurchased and cancelled 68,916 of its common shares at a weighted average price of $0.82 per share pursuant to its NCIB Program. As at June 30, 2025, Orbit Garant had 37,579,840 common shares issued and outstanding.

As at June 30, 2025, the Company's working capital totalled $50.4 million, compared to $48.6 million as at June 30, 2024. Orbit Garant's working capital requirements are primarily related to the funding of inventory and the financing of accounts receivable.

Orbit Garant's audited consolidated financial statements and management's discussion and analysis for Fiscal 2025 are available via the Company's website at www.orbitgarant.com or SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Conference Call

Daniel Maheu, President and CEO, and Pier-Luc Laplante, CFO, will host a conference call for analysts and investors on Thursday, September 25, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. (ET). To join the conference call without operator assistance, you can register and enter your phone number at https://registrations.events/easyconnect/4002554/recfrphaYBpb6dFW4/ to receive an instant automated call back. Alternatively, you can dial 647-932-3411 or 1-800-715-9871 to reach a live operator that will join you into the call.

A live webcast of the call will be available on Orbit Garant's website at: http://www.orbitgarant.com/en/events. The webcast will be archived following conclusion of the call. To access a replay of the conference call dial 647-362-9199 or 1-800-770-2030, passcode: 4002554 #. The replay will be available until October 2, 2025.

RECONCILIATION OF NON - IFRS FINANCIAL MEASURES

Financial data has been prepared in conformity with International Financial Reporting Standards ("IFRS"). However, certain measures used in this discussion and analysis do not have any standardized meaning under IFRS and could be calculated differently by other companies. The Company believes that certain non-IFRS financial measures, when presented in conjunction with comparable IFRS financial measures, are useful to investors and other readers because the information is an appropriate measure to evaluate the Company's operating performance. Internally, the Company uses this non-IFRS financial information as an indicator of business performance. These measures are provided for information purposes, in addition to, and not as a substitute for, measures of financial performance prepared in accordance with IFRS.

--------------------------------------

EBITDA, Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA Margin

Management believes that EBITDA, Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA Margin are important measures when analyzing its operating profitability, as they remove the impact of financing costs, certain non-cash items, income taxes and restructuring costs. As a result, Management considers these measures as useful and comparable benchmarks for evaluating the Company's performance, as companies rarely have the same capital and financing structure.

Reconciliation of EBITDA, Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA Margin

About Orbit Garant

Headquartered in Val-d'Or, Quebec, Orbit Garant is one of the largest Canadian-based mineral drilling companies, providing both underground and surface drilling services in Canada and internationally through its 186 drill rigs and approximately 1,100 employees. Orbit Garant provides services to major, intermediate and junior mining companies, through each stage of mining exploration, development and production. The Company also provides geotechnical drilling services to mining or mineral exploration companies, engineering and environmental consultant firms, and government agencies. For more information, please visit the Company's website at www.orbitgarant.com.

Forward-looking information

This news release may contain forward-looking statements (within the meaning of applicable securities laws) relating to business of Orbit Garant Drilling Inc. (the "Company") and the environment in which it operates. Forward-looking statements are identified by words such as "believe", "anticipate", "expect", "intend", "plan", "will", "may" and other similar expressions. These statements are based on the Company's expectations, estimates, forecasts and projections. They are not guarantees of future performance and involve risks and uncertainties that are difficult to control or predict. Risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results, performance or achievements to differ materially include the world economic climate as it relates to the mining industry; the Canadian economic environment; the Company's ability to attract and retain customers and to manage its assets and operating costs; the political situation in certain jurisdictions in which the Company operates and the operating environment in the jurisdictions in which the Company operates, as well as the risks and uncertainties are discussed in the Company's regulatory filings available at www.sedarplus.ca. There can be no assurance that forward-looking statements will prove to be accurate as actual outcomes and results may differ materially from those expressed in these forward-looking statements. Readers, therefore, should not place undue reliance on any such forward-looking statements. Further, a forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which such statement is made. The Company undertakes no obligation to publicly update any such statement or to reflect new information or the occurrence of future events or circumstances except as required by applicable securities laws.

data.title: UPEXI, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Upexi, Inc., a company operating in the cryptocurrency and consumer products industries, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. This summary highlights the key aspects of the report, offering insights into Upexi's recent activities and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $15.81 million, a decrease of 39% from the previous year, primarily due to the divestment from the recommerce business.
  • Gross Profit: $10.87 million, representing a gross profit margin increase to approximately 67% from 49% in the prior year, excluding digital asset revenue.
  • Loss from Operations: $(12.50) million, reflecting a reduction in operating expenses and strategic focus on higher-margin products.
  • Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Upexi, Inc.: $(13.68) million, an improvement from the previous year's net loss of $(23.66) million, driven by cost reductions and strategic divestitures.
  • Basic Income (Loss) Per Share: $(1.73), compared to $(23.03) in the prior year, reflecting the impact of the reverse stock split and improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Upexi, Inc. operates in the cryptocurrency industry, focusing on managing cash assets through a cryptocurrency portfolio, primarily involving Solana tokens and staking. The company also specializes in the development, manufacturing, and distribution of consumer products.
  • Solana Treasury Strategy: In 2025, Upexi shifted its cash management strategy to include holding digital currency assets, specifically Solana, on its balance sheet. This strategy aims to maximize yield on excess cash by staking Solana tokens.
  • Staking Program: Upexi's staking program involves using Solana tokens to generate returns, with approximately 95% of the SOL treasury staked. The company uses multiple validators to reduce risk and maximize yield.
  • Consumer Products: Upexi owns several brands, including LuckyTail, PRAX, Cure Mushrooms, Moonwalkr, and Gumi Labs, focusing on pet care, energy solutions, functional mushrooms, cannabinoid experiences, and health and wellness products.
  • Distribution and Logistics: Upexi Distribution manages warehousing and logistics, providing services such as storage, consolidation, assembly, and fulfillment for the company's brands and manufacturing operations.
  • Operational Changes: The company divested from the recommerce business to focus on owned brands and white-label products, resulting in a significant decrease in sales from this segment.
  • Future Outlook: Management expects revenue from the digital asset treasury to significantly increase in fiscal year 2026, with a focus on improving gross profit and profit margins.
  • Geographical Operations: The company's operations are primarily based in Florida, with manufacturing and distribution centers supporting its health and wellness products.
  • Employee Base: As of June 30, 2025, Upexi employed 59 full-time employees across its headquarters, manufacturing facility, and distribution warehouse in Florida.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company has strategically shifted its cash management and treasury strategy to include holding digital currency assets, specifically Solana tokens, on its balance sheet. This initiative aims to obtain higher yields on excess cash compared to traditional FDIC-insured accounts. The company has also divested from the recommerce business to focus on owned brands and white-label products, aligning its operations with its strategic focus on digital assets and consumer products.
  • Capital Management: The company raised approximately $92.6 million through a private placement offering, selling shares of common stock and pre-funded warrants. It also secured a $20 million short-term loan collateralized by digital assets, which was later increased to a $50 million credit facility. Additionally, the company completed a 1-for-20 reverse stock split to regain compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements. The company has significantly increased its working capital, primarily due to the liquid Solana held in its digital asset treasury.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates a significant increase in revenue from its digital asset treasury in fiscal year 2026. It plans to continue focusing on improving gross profit margins and increasing revenue from its digital asset strategy. The company expects its general and administrative expenses to stabilize after the initial increase due to capital raising efforts and the implementation of its treasury strategy. The company also plans to maintain sufficient working capital to fund operations and meet debt obligations over the next twelve months.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company faces significant market risks due to the volatility of Solana and other digital assets. The lack of hedging strategies for Solana holdings could lead to substantial financial losses if the value of Solana declines. Regulatory risks are also prominent, as changes in laws governing digital assets could impact the company's operations and financial condition. The potential classification of Solana as a security by regulatory bodies could subject the company to additional compliance requirements and liabilities.
  • Operational Risks: The focus on Solana staking as a primary strategy could expose the company to operational risks if validators underperform or if there are changes in the Solana network that affect staking yields. Cybersecurity threats could lead to the loss of Solana holdings, impacting the company's financial stability. The company's limited operating history in the digital asset space makes it challenging to predict future performance and manage growth effectively. Additionally, the reliance on third-party validators for staking introduces risks related to validator performance and network security.
  • Competitive Risks: The company also faces competitive risks from other entities entering the digital asset space, which could affect market share and pricing strategies. The emergence of central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) and other digital asset initiatives could reduce the demand for Solana, impacting the company's treasury strategy.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the volatile nature of digital assets and the evolving regulatory landscape. The company plans to mitigate these risks by maintaining a diversified validator network for staking and closely monitoring regulatory developments. However, the effectiveness of these strategies remains uncertain given the rapid changes in the digital asset market.

SEC Filing: UPEXI, INC. [ UPXI ] - 10-K - Sep. 24, 2025

data.title: Orbit Garant Drilling 4Q Rev C$47.2M >OGD.T
Orbit Garant Drilling 4Q EPS C$0.06 >OGD.T
Chile to curb budget growth as Codelco ramps up recovery, minister says
data.description:
Key points:
  • 2026 budget growth limited compared to previous years, Grau states
  • Grau confident in Codelco's strategy despite past production challenges
  • Unemployment rate remains a priority for Chile's finance minister

By Fabian Cambero

Chile will have a "responsible" 2026 budget with a limited increase while state-run Codelco, the world's largest copper miner, seeks to recover from recent production problems, the country's new finance minister told Reuters on Wednesday.

In an interview at his office in Santiago, Nicolas Grau, appointed in August by his close ally President Gabriel Boric, said the 2026 budget due in Congress would have a more "limited" spending increase than in recent years.

The specific amount will be announced by Boric, but the budget increase has been on a downward trend since 2023 when it grew 4.2%. Grau said that with this budget, the average annual increase would be about 2%.

"What we're going to do is define a responsible budget with Congress that addresses the country's needs, and provides sufficient flexibility to the next administration," he said. Chile is facing a presidential election in November and Boric, who will serve until March, is not allowed to run for consecutive re-election.

CODELCO'S COPPER RECOVERY

Chile produces about a third of the world's copper, its main source of income. State-owned Codelco has been struggling to boost output for years. In August, it cut its annual production target due to a fatal accident at its flagship El Teniente mine.

Grau emphasized his confidence in Codelco's strategy to close production gaps in previous years, and highlighted initiatives like its partnership with Anglo American to exploit two neighboring deposits.

"(Codelco) is creating these public-private partnerships that allow it to maintain its leadership in the copper sector," Grau said.

SPEEDING UP PERMITTING TO BOOST INVESTMENT

Grau said he was confident the economy would grow 2.5% this year, boosted by an investment increase of more than 5% for carbon-neutral projects like clean energy and digital infrastructure.

Rising unemployment has also been one of Grau's priorities for the final months of the administration. The country's former labor minister, Jeannette Jara, is the leftist government's coalition candidate in the upcoming election.

"Although the unemployment rate has dropped somewhat in the latest data, 8.7% is a challenging unemployment rate. We believe that rate must be reduced," Grau said.

He added that policies like pension reform and improved permitting - a key demand from large miners like BHP and renewable energy companies like TotalEnergies - have allowed for greater economic growth.

Still, he acknowledged that less progress has been made in reforming environmental permitting, a sector that takes the longest compared to other categories.

He argued that the executive branch had made "significant efforts" to move the reform forward but could not predict whether it would be approved during this administration.

data.title: PressLogic Files for IPO, Applied to List on Nasdaq Capital Market Under the Symbol PLAI
